== said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Ihatono Shrine|lt=Ihatono Shrine|WD=Q135039471|1=Ihatono Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県羽島市桑原町八神4665', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛知県一宮市今伊勢町馬寄', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:八剱神社 (羽島市桑原町八神)]] {{wikidata link|Q124496744}} == Japanese content == {{otheruses|Hakken Shrine (Yatsurugi Shrine) in Kuwabara-chō, Hashima City|Hakken Shrine (Hakken Jinja) in Takehana-chō, Hashima City|Hakken Shrine (Takehana-chō, Hashima City)}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Hakken Shrine |Image = [[File:Hakken shrine, Hashima, 2025.jpg|250px]]
Torii |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hashima City|Q819595|WD=Q819595|1=Hashima, Gifu}} Kuwabara-chō Yagami 4665 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ama-no-Iwato-wake-no-Kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|lt=Kushiishimado-no-Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} · {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |Founded = {{ill|lt=Saikō|Q1324664|WD=Q1324664|1=Saikō}} 2 |Main hall = |Other names = Yagami-sama |Pilgrimage sites, etc. = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Hakken Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Hakken Shrine''' (Yatsurugi Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Yagami, Kuwabara-chō, {{ill|lt=Hashima City|Q819595|WD=Q819595|1=Hashima, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is regarded as the tutelary shrine of {{ill|lt=Nakashima District (Gifu Prefecture)|Q24875402|WD=Q24875402|lt=Nakashima District|1=Nakashima district}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} (present-day southern Hashima City). In {{ill|lt=Kanpō|Q1145798|WD=Q1145798|1=Kanpō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1743|Q7580|WD=Q7580|1=1743}}), thirty villages of Nakashima District in Mino Province became its parishioners. ). === Overview === It is one of the candidate shrines (ronsha) for Ishikatana Shrine, an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shrine Register|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of {{ill|lt=Nakashima District|Q780420|WD=Q780420|1=Nakashima district}} in {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}. The place name of this area ({{ill|lt=Yagami Village|Q11391710|WD=Q11391710|lt=Yagami|1=Yagami}}) is derived from Hakken Shrine, and it is said to have been renamed from Ishida in {{ill|lt=Jōō|Q1145081|WD=Q1145081|1=Jōō (Edo period)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1653|Q6965|WD=Q6965|1=1653}}). In {{ill|lt=1931|Q18782|WD=Q18782|1=1931}} (Shōwa 6), it became a gōsha {{Sfn|Hashima City|2004|p=663}}. On October 5, {{ill|lt=1960|Q3754|WD=Q3754|1=1960}} (Shōwa 35), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} (No. Kin 54) by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=83}}. It shared a worship hall with Inari Shrine (enshrined deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}; designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto ShrinesAccording to the on-site information board.{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=83}}), and they existed as separate, independent shrines (Hakken Shrine on the east side and Inari Shrine on the west side). However, in {{ill|lt=2024|Q49619|WD=Q49619|1=2024}} (Reiwa 6) July, Inari Shrine was dissolved as a religious corporation and was merged into Hakken Shrine{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=1200005003539|title=Information on Inari Shrine (National Tax Agency Corporate Number Publication Site)|format= |publisher=National Tax Agency|language=Japanese|accessdate=2025-07-19}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ama-no-Iwato-wake-no-Kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|lt=Kushiishimado-no-Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} === History === Founded in {{ill|lt=Saikō|Q1324664|WD=Q1324664|1=Saikō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=855|Q25073|WD=Q25073|1=855}})[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=279 Hakken Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)]. In Jōō 2 (1653), the Inari Shrine that had been worshipped by the {{ill|lt=Mōri clan (Minamoto lineage)|Q11547665|WD=Q11547665|lt=Mōri Genroku family|1=Mōri Genroku family}} of {{ill|lt=Ichinoeda Village|Q22124456|WD=Q22124456|1=Ichinoeda}} was merged into this shrine. === Subsidiary shrines === * Wakamiya Shrine – transferred here in Jōō 2 (1653) * Shirahige Shrine * Tado Shrine * Fuji Asama Shrine * Konpira Shrine, etc. === Access === * About a 5-minute walk from the “Yagami” bus stop on the southern route of the {{ill|lt=Hashima City Community Bus|Q11610099|WD=Q11610099|1=Hashima City Community Bus}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|ja|editor=Teru Kimura|title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}} * {{Cite book |ja |editor=50th Anniversary History Editorial Committee |title=50-Year History of Hashima City |publisher=Hashima City |date=2004 |ref={{SfnRef|Hashima City|2004}}}} === External links === * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=279 Hakken Shrine] (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=280&shrname=%E2%96%A0%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%96%A0 Inari Shrine] (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yatsurugi Shrine (Yagami, Kuwabara-cho, Hashima City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatsurugi Shrine (Yagami, Kuwabara-cho, Hashima City)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hashima]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hashima]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

address (P6375) == * {'text': '岐阜県羽島市桑原町八神4665', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '愛知県一宮市今伊勢町馬寄', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:八剱神社 (羽島市桑原町八神)]] {{wikidata link|Q124496744}} == Japanese content == {{otheruses|Hakken Shrine (Yatsurugi Shrine) in Kuwabara-chō, Hashima City|Hakken Shrine (Hakken Jinja) in Takehana-chō, Hashima City|Hakken Shrine (Takehana-chō, Hashima City)}} {{Shinto shrine |Name = Hakken Shrine |Image = [[File:Hakken shrine, Hashima, 2025.jpg|250px]]
Torii |Location = {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hashima City|Q819595|WD=Q819595|1=Hashima, Gifu}} Kuwabara-chō Yagami 4665 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Ama-no-Iwato-wake-no-Kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|lt=Kushiishimado-no-Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} |Shrine rank = Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} · {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} |Founded = {{ill|lt=Saikō|Q1324664|WD=Q1324664|1=Saikō}} 2 |Main hall = |Other names = Yagami-sama |Pilgrimage sites, etc. = |Annual festival = |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Hakken Shrine|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Hakken Shrine''' (Yatsurugi Jinja) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Yagami, Kuwabara-chō, {{ill|lt=Hashima City|Q819595|WD=Q819595|1=Hashima, Gifu}}, {{ill|lt=Gifu Prefecture|Q131277|WD=Q131277|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. It is regarded as the tutelary shrine of {{ill|lt=Nakashima District (Gifu Prefecture)|Q24875402|WD=Q24875402|lt=Nakashima District|1=Nakashima district}} in {{ill|lt=Mino Province|Q864156|WD=Q864156|1=Mino Province}} (present-day southern Hashima City). In {{ill|lt=Kanpō|Q1145798|WD=Q1145798|1=Kanpō}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1743|Q7580|WD=Q7580|1=1743}}), thirty villages of Nakashima District in Mino Province became its parishioners. ). === Overview === It is one of the candidate shrines (ronsha) for Ishikatana Shrine, an {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shrine Register|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} of {{ill|lt=Nakashima District|Q780420|WD=Q780420|1=Nakashima district}} in {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}. The place name of this area ({{ill|lt=Yagami Village|Q11391710|WD=Q11391710|lt=Yagami|1=Yagami}}) is derived from Hakken Shrine, and it is said to have been renamed from Ishida in {{ill|lt=Jōō|Q1145081|WD=Q1145081|1=Jōō (Edo period)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1653|Q6965|WD=Q6965|1=1653}}). In {{ill|lt=1931|Q18782|WD=Q18782|1=1931}} (Shōwa 6), it became a gōsha {{Sfn|Hashima City|2004|p=663}}. On October 5, {{ill|lt=1960|Q3754|WD=Q3754|1=1960}} (Shōwa 35), it was designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|1=Kinpei-sha}} (No. Kin 54) by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=83}}. It shared a worship hall with Inari Shrine (enshrined deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}}; designated a {{ill|lt=Kinpei-sha|Q119929592|WD=Q119929592|lt=Ginpei-sha|1=Kinpei-sha}} by the Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto ShrinesAccording to the on-site information board.{{sfn|Kimura|1997|p=83}}), and they existed as separate, independent shrines (Hakken Shrine on the east side and Inari Shrine on the west side). However, in {{ill|lt=2024|Q49619|WD=Q49619|1=2024}} (Reiwa 6) July, Inari Shrine was dissolved as a religious corporation and was merged into Hakken Shrine{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/henkorireki-johoto.html?selHouzinNo=1200005003539|title=Information on Inari Shrine (National Tax Agency Corporate Number Publication Site)|format= |publisher=National Tax Agency|language=Japanese|accessdate=2025-07-19}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ama-no-Iwato-wake-no-Kami|Q10940542|WD=Q10940542|lt=Kushiishimado-no-Mikoto|1=Ama-no-Iwato-wake no Kami}} * {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Uka-no-Mitama-no-Kami|1=Ukanomitama}} === History === Founded in {{ill|lt=Saikō|Q1324664|WD=Q1324664|1=Saikō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=855|Q25073|WD=Q25073|1=855}})[https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=279 Hakken Shrine (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines)]. In Jōō 2 (1653), the Inari Shrine that had been worshipped by the {{ill|lt=Mōri clan (Minamoto lineage)|Q11547665|WD=Q11547665|lt=Mōri Genroku family|1=Mōri Genroku family}} of {{ill|lt=Ichinoeda Village|Q22124456|WD=Q22124456|1=Ichinoeda}} was merged into this shrine. === Subsidiary shrines === * Wakamiya Shrine – transferred here in Jōō 2 (1653) * Shirahige Shrine * Tado Shrine * Fuji Asama Shrine * Konpira Shrine, etc. === Access === * About a 5-minute walk from the “Yagami” bus stop on the southern route of the {{ill|lt=Hashima City Community Bus|Q11610099|WD=Q11610099|1=Hashima City Community Bus}}. === Notes === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|ja|editor=Teru Kimura|title=Tour of Major Shrines in Gifu Prefecture|publisher=Nagara Tenjin Shrine|date=1997|ref={{sfnref|Kimura|1997}}}} * {{Cite book |ja |editor=50th Anniversary History Editorial Committee |title=50-Year History of Hashima City |publisher=Hashima City |date=2004 |ref={{SfnRef|Hashima City|2004}}}} === External links === * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=279 Hakken Shrine] (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) * [https://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=280&shrname=%E2%96%A0%E7%A8%B2%E8%8D%B7%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E2%96%A0 Inari Shrine] (Gifu Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Yatsurugi Shrine (Yagami, Kuwabara-cho, Hashima City)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Yatsurugi Shrine (Yagami, Kuwabara-cho, Hashima City)]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hashima]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hashima]]
